summary: Do not open R-2–R-6 yet: R-1 omits an existing temp-name producer, so the planned single-owner deletion test cannot be completed on this seam.

findings.title: The temp-path seam cannot absorb the atomic writer
findings.body: `TMP_PREFIX` is private and the only authoring API hard-wires it to `root/.objects`. However, the plan's deletion evidence explicitly identifies `src/store/atomic.rs:8` as another `.tmp-` producer, and that writer creates temporary files in `.objects` when reconcile persists `.gc-pending.json`. No remaining increment migrates this producer, although R-2 requires `.tmp-` to disappear from `src/store`. The later work must therefore either leave the layout module non-deep and fail its acceptance condition, or reopen the skeleton interface. It also leaves a drift path where changing Layout's prefix makes interrupted atomic-write files classify as `Other` and evade temp cleanup.
findings.recommendation: Add a root-independent temp filename or sibling-path authoring operation to the layout seam, make `temp_blob_path` delegate to it, and explicitly include `atomic::write_atomic` as a real second consumer in R-2 while preserving the existing `.tmp-<unique>` bytes.

next_steps: Revise the R-1 seam and R-2 increment plan, then rerun the structure review before opening the remaining DAG.
